title: Holles Street (The Rocky Road To Dublin)
content:
Through the air,
        Everywhere, the rain is falling;
        Brawling on house and tree:
        On every place that you can see
        The rain drops go;
        The roofs are wet, the walls, the ground below.

        Midnight has come;
        Now all the people stretch them blind and dumb
        Each in a bed
        Save I, who sit and listen overhead
        Unto the rain
        Splashing upon the roof and window-pane.

        Midnight! and I
        Can get no sleep, nor can the sky.
meaning:
This evocative piece by James Stephens, titled "Holles Street (The Rocky Road To Dublin)", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
